info.title: jadler compilation errors fix with JavaParser

parts.text: "Project @jadler/ does not compile. Plan: 1) Run mvn compile in the project @jadler/ to get the compilation errors only. 2) Generate a JavaParser source code transformation to fix the errors.    - Use the project in folder @/workspace/javaparser-base-template/ as the base project template.    - Use JavaParser AST manipulation to create the transformation.    - Save the transformation rules inside the folder @/workspace/javaparser-base-template/, e.g., in /workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the JavaParser API documentation located in folder /workspace/javaparser-api-docs/ for reference. 3) Ensure the generated transformation compiles correctly. 4) Apply the transformation to fix the compilation errors. 5) Verify that the project now compiles successfully with mvn compile. "

Let me break down this task:
1. Run `mvn compile` in `/workspace/jadler/` to get compilation errors
2. Create a JavaParser transformation to fix the errors using the template in `/workspace/javaparser-base-template/`
3. Use JavaParser API docs in `/workspace/javaparser-api-docs/` for reference
4. Ensure the transformation compiles
5. Apply the transformation to fix jadler compilation errors
6. Verify jadler compiles successfully

Let me start by exploring the directories and running the initial compile to see the errors.

parts.text:
The compilation errors are clear:
- Source option 6 is no longer supported. Use 7 or later.
- Target option 6 is no longer supported. Use 7 or later.

This is a Java version compatibility issue. The jadler project is configured to use Java 6, but the current JDK doesn't support it.

Now I need to:
1. Look at the javaparser-base-template to understand its structure
2. Create a JavaParser transformation that modifies the pom.xml files to change the Java version from 6 to a newer version (like 8 or 11)
3. Apply the transformation

Let me explore the template structure and the jadler pom.xml files.

There are additional compilation errors in jadler-jetty related to Jetty API changes. The project was written for an older version of Jetty, and the API has changed. Let me check the jadler-jetty pom.xml and the source files to understand the issue better.

The errors are:
1. `package org.eclipse.jetty.server.nio does not exist` - SelectChannelConnector is from old Jetty
2. `cannot find symbol: method setSendServerVersion(boolean)` - API changed
3. `cannot find symbol: method setSendDateHeader(boolean)` - API changed
4. `cannot find symbol: class SelectChannelConnector` - class removed in newer Jetty
5. `cannot find symbol: method setPort(int)` - API changed
6. `cannot find symbol: method getLocalPort()` - API changed
7. `JadlerHandler is not abstract and does not override abstract method handle...` - API changed

These are API compatibility issues with Jetty. The project uses Jetty 11.0.7 (defined in the parent pom), but the code was written for an older Jetty version (probably Jetty 8 or 9).

<path>/workspace/jadler/jadler-jetty/src/main/java/net/jadler/stubbing/server/jetty/JettyStubHttpServer.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: /*
2:  * Copyright (c) 2012 - 2016 Jadler contributors
3:  * This program is made available under the terms of the MIT License.
4:  */
5: package net.jadler.stubbing.server.jetty;
6: 
7: import net.jadler.RequestManager;
8: import net.jadler.stubbing.server.StubHttpServer;
9: import org.eclipse.jetty.server.Server;
10: import org.slf4j.Logger;
11: import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;
12: import org.apache.commons.lang.Validate;
13: import org.eclipse.jetty.server.Connector;
14: import org.eclipse.jetty.server.nio.SelectChannelConnector;
15: 
16: 
17: /**
18:  * Default stub http server implementation using Jetty as an http server.
19:  */
20: public class JettyStubHttpServer implements StubHttpServer {
21: 
22:     private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(JettyStubHttpServer.class);
23:     private final Server server;
24:     private final Connector httpConnector;
25: 
26:     public JettyStubHttpServer() {
27:         this(0);
28:     }
29:     
30: 
31:     public JettyStubHttpServer(final int port) {
32:         this.server = new Server();
33:         this.server.setSendServerVersion(false);
34:         this.server.setSendDateHeader(true);
35:         
36:         this.httpConnector = new SelectChannelConnector();
37:         this.httpConnector.setPort(port);
38:         server.addConnector(this.httpConnector);
39:     }
40:     
41: 
42:     /**
43:      * {@inheritDoc}
44:      */
45:     @Override
46:     public void registerRequestManager(final RequestManager ruleProvider) {
47:         Validate.notNull(ruleProvider, "ruleProvider cannot be null");
48: 
49:         server.setHandler(new JadlerHandler(ruleProvider));
50:     }
51:     
52: 
53:     /**
54:      * {@inheritDoc}
55:      */
56:     @Override
57:     public void start() throws Exception {
58:         logger.debug("starting jetty");
59:         server.start();
60:         logger.debug("jetty started");
61:     }
62: 
63: 
64:     /**
65:      * {@inheritDoc}
66:      */
67:     @Override
68:     public void stop() throws Exception {
69:         logger.debug("stopping jetty");
70:         server.stop();
71:         logger.debug("jetty stopped");
72:     }
73:     
74: 
75:     /**
76:      * {@inheritDoc}
77:      */
78:     @Override
79:     public int getPort() {
80:         return httpConnector.getLocalPort();
81:     }
82: }

(End of file - total 82 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: /*
2:  * Copyright (c) 2012 - 2016 Jadler contributors
3:  * This program is made available under the terms of the MIT License.
4:  */
5: package net.jadler.stubbing.server.jetty;
6: 
7: import net.jadler.stubbing.StubResponse;
8: import net.jadler.RequestManager;
9: import org.eclipse.jetty.server.Request;
10: import org.eclipse.jetty.server.handler.AbstractHandler;
11: import javax.servlet.ServletException;
12: import javax.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest;
13: import javax.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;
14: import java.io.IOException;
15: import java.io.OutputStream;
16: import net.jadler.KeyValues;
17: import org.apache.commons.lang.Validate;
18: 
19: 
20: /**
21:  * Jetty Handler which handles all http communication by returning an http response according to a stub response
22:  * obtained from a {@link RequestManager} instance.
23:  */
24: class JadlerHandler extends AbstractHandler {
25: 
26:     private final RequestManager requestManager;
27: 
28:     
29:     /**
30:      * @param requestManager request manager instance to retrieve stub responses
31:      */
32:     JadlerHandler(final RequestManager requestManager) {
33:         Validate.notNull(requestManager, "requestManager cannot be null");
34:         this.requestManager = requestManager;
35:     }
36: 
37: 
38:     @Override
39:     public void handle(final String target, final Request baseRequest, final HttpServletRequest request,
40:             final HttpServletResponse response) throws IOException, ServletException {
41: 
42:         final net.jadler.Request req = RequestUtils.convert(request);
43:         final StubResponse stubResponse = this.requestManager.provideStubResponseFor(req);
44:         
45:         response.setStatus(stubResponse.getStatus());
46:         this.insertResponseHeaders(stubResponse.getHeaders(), response);        
47:         
48:         baseRequest.setHandled(true);
49:         
50:         this.processDelay(stubResponse.getDelay());
51:         this.insertResponseBody(stubResponse.getBody(), response);
52:     }
53: 
54:     
55:     private void insertResponseBody(final byte[] body, final HttpServletResponse response) throws IOException {
56:         if (body.length > 0) {
57:             final OutputStream os = response.getOutputStream();
58:             os.write(body);
59:         }
60:     }
61: 
62:     
63:     private void insertResponseHeaders(final KeyValues headers, final HttpServletResponse response) {
64:         for (final String key: headers.getKeys()) {
65:             
66:             for (final String value: headers.getValues(key)) {
67:                 response.addHeader(key, value);
68:             }
69:         }
70:     }
71: 
72: 
73:     private void processDelay(final long delay) {
74:         if (delay > 0) {
75:             try {
76:                 Thread.sleep(delay);
77:             } catch (final InterruptedException e) {
78:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
79:             }
80:         }
81:     }
82: }

(End of file - total 82 lines)

<path>/workspace/jadler/jadler-jetty/src/main/java/net/jadler/stubbing/server/jetty/JadlerHandler.java</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: /*
2:  * Copyright (c) 2012 - 2016 Jadler contributors
3:  * This program is made available under the terms of the MIT License.
4:  */
5: package net.jadler.stubbing.server.jetty;
6: 
7: import net.jadler.stubbing.StubResponse;
8: import net.jadler.RequestManager;
9: import org.eclipse.jetty.server.Request;
10: import org.eclipse.jetty.server.handler.AbstractHandler;
11: import javax.servlet.ServletException;
12: import javax.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest;
13: import javax.servlet.http.HttpServletResponse;
14: import java.io.IOException;
15: import java.io.OutputStream;
16: import net.jadler.KeyValues;
17: import org.apache.commons.lang.Validate;
18: 
19: 
20: /**
21:  * Jetty Handler which handles all http communication by returning an http response according to a stub response
22:  * obtained from a {@link RequestManager} instance.
23:  */
24: class JadlerHandler extends AbstractHandler {
25: 
26:     private final RequestManager requestManager;
27: 
28:     
29:     /**
30:      * @param requestManager request manager instance to retrieve stub responses
31:      */
32:     JadlerHandler(final RequestManager requestManager) {
33:         Validate.notNull(requestManager, "requestManager cannot be null");
34:         this.requestManager = requestManager;
35:     }
36: 
37: 
38:     @Override
39:     public void handle(String target, Request baseRequest, HttpServletRequest request,
40:             HttpServletResponse response) throws IOException, ServletException {
41: 
42:         final net.jadler.Request req = RequestUtils.convert(request);
43:         final StubResponse stubResponse = this.requestManager.provideStubResponseFor(req);
44:         
45:         response.setStatus(stubResponse.getStatus());
46:         this.insertResponseHeaders(stubResponse.getHeaders(), response);        
47:         
48:         baseRequest.setHandled(true);
49:         
50:         this.processDelay(stubResponse.getDelay());
51:         this.insertResponseBody(stubResponse.getBody(), response);
52:     }
53: 
54:     
55:     private void insertResponseBody(final byte[] body, final HttpServletResponse response) throws IOException {
56:         if (body.length > 0) {
57:             final OutputStream os = response.getOutputStream();
58:             os.write(body);
59:         }
60:     }
61: 
62:     
63:     private void insertResponseHeaders(final KeyValues headers, final HttpServletResponse response) {
64:         for (final String key: headers.getKeys()) {
65:             
66:             for (final String value: headers.getValues(key)) {
67:                 response.addHeader(key, value);
68:             }
69:         }
70:     }
71: 
72: 
73:     private void processDelay(final long delay) {
74:         if (delay > 0) {
75:             try {
76:                 Thread.sleep(delay);
77:             } catch (final InterruptedException e) {
78:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
79:             }
80:         }
81:     }
82: }

(End of file - total 82 lines)
</content>

<content>1: /*
2:  * Copyright (c) 2012 - 2016 Jadler contributors
3:  * This program is made available under the terms of the MIT License.
4:  */
5: package net.jadler.stubbing.server.jetty;
6: 
7: import net.jadler.Request;
8: import javax.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest;
9: import java.io.IOException;
10: import java.net.URI;
11: import java.nio.charset.Charset;
12: import java.util.Enumeration;
13: 
14: import static org.apache.commons.io.IOUtils.toByteArray;
15: import static org.apache.commons.lang.StringUtils.isNotBlank;
16: 
17: 
18: /**
19:  * Converts {@link javax.servlet.http.HttpServletRequest} to {@link net.jadler.stubbing.Request}.
20:  */
21: class RequestUtils {
22:     
23:     static Request convert(final HttpServletRequest source) throws IOException {
24:         
25:         final Charset encoding = isNotBlank(source.getCharacterEncoding())
26:                 ? Charset.forName(source.getCharacterEncoding())
27:                 : null;
28:         
29:         final Request.Builder builder = Request.builder()
30:                 .method(source.getMethod())
31:                 .requestURI(URI.create(source.getRequestURL() + getQueryString(source)))
32:                 .body(toByteArray(source.getInputStream()));
33:         
34:         if (encoding != null) {
35:             builder.encoding(encoding);
36:         }
37:         
38:         return addHeaders(builder, source).build();
39:     }
40:     
41: 
42:     private static String getQueryString(final HttpServletRequest source) {
43:         return source.getQueryString() != null ? ("?" + source.getQueryString()) : "";
44:     }
45: 
46:     
47:     private static Request.Builder addHeaders(final Request.Builder builder, final HttpServletRequest req) {
48:         @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
49:         Enumeration<String> names = req.getHeaderNames();
50:         
51:         while (names.hasMoreElements()) {
52:             final String name = names.nextElement();
53:             
54:             @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
55:             final Enumeration<String> values = req.getHeaders(name);
56:             
57:             while (values.hasMoreElements()) {
58:                 builder.header(name, values.nextElement());
59:             }
60:         }        
61:         
62:         return builder;
63:     }
64: }

(End of file - total 64 lines)
